title: lysimachia daphnoides
intro: Lysimachia daphnoides, also known as Daphne-berry or White-barren, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, belonging to the family Primulaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Lysimachia daphnoides is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
intro_study: According to "Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica", Lysimachia daphnoides has been used in the treatment of fever, ulcers, diarrhea, as an analgesic, and as an anti-inflammatory agent.
